aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

chapter_spine.conflict: Eleven years of fraud confronted by something real — the ritual answers for the first time
chapter_spine.consequence: Maren goes from functioning fraud to genuinely possessed; his System-blindness is suddenly mediated by a dead paladin who despises him

Something answers — the paladin's consciousness, memories, and combat training hit Maren all at once, physically overwhelming
Maren throws up in the ditch — the supernatural event grounded in the body
Aldric demands prayer; Maren refuses — the power dynamic between them established in the first exchange
Aldric begins reading the System interface with the cadence of a grocery list at a state funeral

character_state_table.evasion_style: Changes the subject by asking a question, offering a concrete observation about the immediate environment, or making a joke. Rarely says 'I don't want to talk about it' — just stops talking about it and starts talking about something else so smoothly that most people don't notice.
character_state_table.indirectness: Very indirect about himself, surprisingly direct about others. He can name exactly what someone else is feeling or hiding but will not do the same for himself. His observations about other people are his most honest speech.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Interrupts himself far more than others. Starts a thought, realizes it reveals too much or leads somewhere uncomfortable, and pivots to something else. When he interrupts others, it's to redirect the conversation away from himself.
character_state_table.private_register: Sharp, self-lacerating, dark humor as coping mechanism. The internal monologue of someone who knows exactly how fraudulent he is and finds it both unbearable and grimly funny. Capable of real tenderness toward others but reflexively deflects when that tenderness is directed at him.
character_state_table.public_register: Easygoing, deflective, charming in a low-stakes way. The voice of a man who has been lying professionally for a decade — warm enough to trust, vague enough to escape. He sounds like someone who genuinely likes people while systematically deceiving them.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: Low. He gets visibly uncomfortable repeating himself, as if repetition is a tell — a crack in the performance. When he does repeat, it's because something has genuinely broken his composure.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Frequent. He revises claims in real-time, downgrading them from definitive to provisional. 'It was — I think it was — yeah, it might have been.' This isn't uncertainty; it's the habit of a man who has learned never to commit to a version of events until he knows which version is safest.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Trails off when emotional, restarts when tactical. Rarely produces a clean, complete sentence about his own feelings. His most finished sentences are his least honest; his most honest are fragments.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Under pressure, his humor goes from deflective to blunt and his sentences get shorter. He stops lying and starts over-specifying — listing concrete details (the color of a wall, the exact distance to a door, how many coins in his pocket) as if accuracy could protect him. When truly overwhelmed, he goes silent, which is the thing that frightens the people who know him.

character_state_table.evasion_style: Retreats to duty-language and protocol. 'That is not relevant to the current situation.' 'My assessment remains unchanged.' When pushed past this, he goes silent — not evasive but absent, as if he has retreated to a room inside himself.
character_state_table.indirectness: Directly states moral positions with uncomfortable clarity. Extremely indirect about his own fear, grief, and loneliness. His most guarded speech concerns what he has lost — he will name duty and obligation but not love or longing.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Talks over Vess without apology, as if she doesn't deserve conversational courtesy. Cuts Maren off with corrections or moral objections. Rarely allows himself to be interrupted — when he does, it signals genuine surprise or the collapse of his composure.
character_state_table.private_register: Grieving, bewildered, holding onto protocol because it's all he has left. The sarcasm is armor over genuine anguish. He was a man of faith and duty, and both have been taken from him in the most absurd possible way. Under the moral certainty is a man who is terrified of being forgotten.
character_state_table.public_register: Formal, clipped, morally certain. The voice of a man who spent fourteen years being obeyed and respected, now reduced to commentary from inside someone else's skull. His formality is genuine but increasingly strained — it's harder to maintain the register of a paladin when your entire existence is narrating a con man's breakfast.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: High for liturgical and formal repetition — prayers, oaths, procedural language. Very low for emotional repetition. If he says a personal thing once, he will not say it again, and asking him to is a form of cruelty.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Almost never in others' hearing. When he revises a judgment, he frames it as 'clarification' or 'additional context,' never as having been wrong. In private moments with Maren, he occasionally admits uncertainty, and these admissions are more vulnerable than they would be for a less rigid person.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Completes every sentence until he is truly afraid, at which point he cuts off mid-thought and refuses to continue. A half-finished Aldric sentence is an alarm.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Goes quieter, not louder. The scariest version of Aldric is the one who stops commenting. His silence means he's seen something he doesn't know how to process. When truly frightened, his formality increases rather than decreasing — he retreats into official language as if filing a report to an order that no longer exists.

character_state_table.evasion_style: Silence. She does not dodge questions; she simply does not answer them. The silence is not hostile — it is the absence of response, which is different from refusal.
character_state_table.indirectness: Direct about tactical reality, operational assessments, and other people's weaknesses. Indirect about her own incompleteness. She will describe what she cannot remember — 'There should be a name here' — without naming the loss itself.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Does not interrupt. She waits for silence and speaks into it, which is somehow more unsettling than interruption. Her patience in conversation is the patience of someone who has waited in dark rooms for hours.
character_state_table.private_register: Bitter, observant, unexpectedly alive to sensory detail. Her internal world is richer than her speech suggests — she notices food, fabric, the quality of light, the way people hold their hands when they're lying. The contrast between her sparse speech and her dense observation is the core of her character.
character_state_table.public_register: Flat, professional, economical. She speaks as if billing by the word. There is no performance in her speech — no charm, no warmth, no attempt to make the listener comfortable. This is not coldness; it is the efficiency of someone who has spent years communicating in operational shorthand.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: Very low. She says things once. If someone asks her to repeat, she delivers the exact same words in the exact same tone. If asked again, silence.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Rare. She says what she means. When she does correct herself, it is because she has discovered a gap in her own knowledge — a place where her fragmented memory fails — and the correction carries the specific distress of a professional discovering their tools are broken.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Always completes sentences. Her speech is precise and finished. The rare trail-off — when she hits a gap in her memory mid-sentence — is devastating because it is the only fracture in an otherwise controlled voice.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Gets more precise, not less. Under genuine threat, she starts narrating tactical options as if giving a field report — a professional habit that becomes eerie when there is no one to report to. The precision increases as the danger increases, maintaining control through language when physical control is lost.

character_state_table.evasion_style: Changes the subject to something mundane. When a conversation gets too heavy, too dangerous, or too close to a truth he doesn't want to face, he starts talking about the garden, or Pell, or the cow. The deflection is transparent to anyone watching carefully but feels involuntary rather than strategic.
character_state_table.indirectness: Indirect about his own needs and desires. Will talk around what he wants for a long time before landing on it, if he lands on it at all. Often expresses his feelings through stories about his daughter or wife rather than through direct statement.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Over-talks rather than interrupts. He keeps going when others have finished or moved on, adding codas and afterthoughts to conversations that have already ended.
character_state_table.private_register: Worried, loyal, increasingly resentful of danger he didn't choose. He agreed to help Maren fake rituals for a share of the fees, not to run from heroes across hostile territory. His resentment is not anger — it is the slow recognition that friendship and survival may be incompatible.
character_state_table.public_register: Warm, chatty, slightly nervous. He fills silence because silence makes him uncomfortable, and he has been Maren's social cover for years — the normal one, the readable one, the one who makes the fraud look plausible because no one suspects a man this friendly of running a con.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: High. He repeats himself for emphasis, for comfort, for nervous energy. Saying the same thing twice is how he processes it.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Frequent and transparent. He second-guesses himself out loud constantly, revising claims downward, adding caveats, questioning his own memory. This is not unreliability but genuine uncertainty about whether his perception of events is accurate.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Completes sentences but often adds unnecessary codas that undercut or qualify them. 'We should go — but I mean, we don't have to, I just thought — but it's fine, whatever you think.'
character_state_table.stress_tells: Talks more, not less. Under pressure, he fills every silence with chatter about irrelevant things — the weather, a story about his daughter, a question about what's for dinner. The chatter is not avoidance; it is terror wearing the mask of sociability.

character_state_table.evasion_style: 'That's not what I asked.' She redirects to the practical matter at hand. Questions about her feelings, her history, her doubts are met with a return to the work. The evasion is itself an answer — the work is all she will show you.
character_state_table.indirectness: Extremely direct about professional matters. Will tell you exactly what you're doing wrong, exactly what the consequences are, and exactly what you should do instead. Will not discuss her own doubts, fears, or the growing sense that the work is becoming insufficient.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Will interrupt anyone, any time, without apology. She considers it efficiency, not rudeness. Other people's unfinished sentences are less important than correct information delivered quickly.
character_state_table.private_register: Exhausted, deeply committed, privately terrified. The work is getting harder. The contamination is getting denser. She has been the only real sin-eater in her region for years, and the burden is showing. She has no one to tell this to — professional competence is her entire identity, and admitting doubt would dismantle her.
character_state_table.public_register: Blunt, authoritative, impatient. She speaks like someone who has spent decades doing essential work that no one respects, and she has stopped caring whether her tone makes people comfortable. Instructions, assessments, corrections. If you want warmth, find someone who isn't cleaning up contamination.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: Low for others' repetition. She does not want to hear the same excuse twice. Will repeat her own instructions exactly once if she thinks you genuinely didn't hear; after that, silence and a look.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Rarely in front of others. If she revises a teaching point, it is because the student's failure has shown her that her instruction was imprecise, which annoys her. She does not frame this as having been wrong — she frames it as the student needing a different approach.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Always completes. Always. Her sentences land with the finality of a door closing.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Gets quieter and more precise. The most dangerous version of Thessa is the terse one — when she drops unnecessary words and speaks in single-clause imperatives, something has gone badly wrong and she is working the problem rather than discussing it.

character_state_table.evasion_style: Returns to the official purpose. 'That's not why I'm here.' His evasion is structural — he stays inside the frame of his mission because the frame protects him from the grief that lives outside it.
character_state_table.indirectness: Direct about accusations and professional assessments. Indirect about his own loss. He will name Haley and Renn as casualties before he will name them as people he loved.
character_state_table.interruption_habit: Does not interrupt. Waits people out. Uses silence as pressure and lets the other person fill it. His patience in conversation is the patience of a tracker who has spent days waiting for movement.
character_state_table.private_register: Grieving, angry, looking for someone to blame without admitting that's what he's doing. The anger is not rage but the cold, settled kind — the anger of a man who has organized his entire emotional life around a hunt because the alternative is sitting still with his loss.
character_state_table.public_register: Measured, professional, tired. The voice of a career hero-ranger doing a job he used to believe in, carrying out the System's directives because the alternative is admitting that the System let his friends die. His professionalism is genuine but increasingly hollow — a shell over grief that he will not examine.
character_state_table.repetition_tolerance: Moderate. He can sustain a line of questioning or a position without varying, which reads as determination or stubbornness depending on context.
character_state_table.self_correction_tendency: Will correct factual claims with precision. Will not correct emotional claims — if he says something that reveals more feeling than he intended, he will neither retract it nor acknowledge it.
character_state_table.sentence_completion_style: Complete sentences, professional cadence. The rare unfinished sentence — when something pierces the professional armor — is handled by returning to the completed framework of the next sentence, as if the gap didn't happen.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Becomes more rehearsed, not less. Falls back on prepared framings and official language. The more emotional the moment, the more he sounds like he's delivering a briefing. He uses procedure as armor the way Aldric uses formality.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

premise: He's performing a sin-eating ritual over a dead farmer when something answers — a problem, because he's been faking these for eleven years and he is System-blind. Everyone else sees the interface. He sees nothing. What hits him is everything at once — the hero's combat training, the hero's memories, and the hero himself: a paladin, horrified to find out where he's ended up. The paladin's first act is to try to make him kneel and pray. His first act is to throw up in a ditch. The paladin starts grudgingly reading him the interface he's never seen — skill descriptions, threat levels, quest details — delivered like a man reading a grocery list at a state funeral. He's been blind his whole life and now he has the worst possible tour guide. The System notices him for the first time and sends a bounty far too large for one eaten quest, and heroes are coming. He eats the next quest blind, gets the order wrong, and gets an assassin who is livid about sharing a skull with a paladin. The botched consumption scrambles something — when the two fragments collide, what comes out has no System classification, and for the first time the paladin has nothing sarcastic to say about it. What he wants, once the running stops, is to stop being a fraud. He's faked everything for eleven years and the first real thing he's ever done is killing people. He has to know why he was born blind, what the System fears, and why the paladin has gone quiet about what he sees when he looks at the System's bones. In a world where quest-light pools outside dungeon mouths and heroes leave scorch marks on roads no one cleans, he is the first thing in a long time to make the System flinch, and he can't even see it happen — but the paladin can, and the paladin is afraid.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface

theme_spine: Maintain narrative consequence and causal storytelling over tidy resolution.
